page.title: Participle Clauses
page.subtitle_html: Participle Clauses - C1 present, past, and perfect participle clauses for reason, sequence, background action, and reduced relatives.
page.subtitle_text: C1 present, past, and perfect participle clauses for reason, sequence, background action, and reduced relatives.

blocks.body: {"level":"C1","intro":"Participle Clauses: C1 anchors for reducing clauses and showing reason, sequence, passive meaning, or background action.","rules":[{"label":"Present participle","text":"Use -ing clauses when the subject performs the action or when giving background information.","example":"Having little time, she finished only the introduction."},{"label":"Past participle","text":"Use V3 clauses for passive meanings and reduced relative clauses.","example":"Written in a hurry, the report contained several errors."},{"label":"Perfect participle","text":"Use having + V3 for an action completed before the main action.","example":"Having reviewed the evidence, the committee changed its decision."}]}

blocks.heading: C1 focus
blocks.body: This compact page exists as the theory anchor for the English Sentence Builder C1 participle clauses lesson package.
